leadText: Venipede (Japanese: フシデ Fushide) is a Bug/Poison Pokémon introduced in Generation V. It evolves into Whirlipede starting at level 22, which evolves into Scolipede starting at level 30.

sections.title: Biology
sections.text: Biology [ edit | edit source ] Venipede is an insectoid Pokémon with a pronounced hump on the upper portion of its body. Its thorax and head are mainly magenta with black markings that consist of rings on its shoulders, a band around each antenna, and an upside-down 'Y' on its neck. A prominent ridge leading down to a pointed tip splits the top half of its head, and its lower jaw is black. Its fangs are known to be highly venomous. Venipede has round, heavy-lidded eyes that bulge away from the head slightly. Venipede's dark green abdomen is segmented, and it has four pairs of stubby black legs. Two short feelers banded with black extend from its hindquarters, which it uses in addition to its antennae to explore its surroundings and detect prey by sensing air vibrations. Venipede is highly aggressive for its size, and its bite injects a poison strong enough to paralyze large bird Pokémon. It often gets into fights with Sizzlipede , a related species. Venipede tends to prey on any Pokémon it thinks it can eat without considering its actions thoroughly. Evolution [ edit | edit source ] Venipede evolves into Whirlipede , which evolves into Scolipede . (For specifics on this Pokémon's Evolution in the games, refer to Game data→Evolution data .) Unevolved Venipede Bug Poison → First Evolution Whirlipede Bug Poison → Second Evolution Scolipede Bug Poison

Generation V Unova B W : #049 Unova B2 W2 : #043 Black Its bite injects a potent poison, enough to paralyze large bird Pokémon that try to prey on it. White It discovers what is going on around it by using the feelers on its head and tail. It is brutally aggressive. Black 2 Using the feelers on its head and tail, it picks up vibrations in the air to determine its prey's location and state. White 2 Generation VI Central Kalos #120 Coastal Kalos #— Mountain Kalos #— Hoenn #— X It discovers what is going on around it by using the feelers on its head and tail. It is brutally aggressive. Y Its bite injects a potent poison, enough to paralyze large bird Pokémon that try to prey on it. Omega Ruby It discovers what is going on around it by using the feelers on its head and tail. Generation VII Alola #— Kanto #— This Pokémon has no Pokédex entries in Generation VII . Generation VIII Galar #— Isle of Armor #074 Crown Tundra #— Sinnoh #— Hisui #— This Pokémon has no Pokédex entries in Brilliant Diamond, Shining Pearl , and Legends: Arceus . Sword Venipede and Sizzlipede are similar species, but when the two meet, a huge fight ensues. Shield Its fangs are highly venomous. If this Pokémon finds prey it thinks it can eat, it leaps for them without any thought of how things might turn out. Generation IX Paldea #— Kitakami #— Blueberry #— Lumiose #068 Hyperspace #— This Pokémon has no Pokédex entries in Pokémon Scarlet and Violet . Legends: Z-A Its bite injects a venom potent enough to paralyze even the large bird Pokémon that try to prey on it. Spin-off games Pokopia #— This Pokémon has no Pokédex entries in Pokopia . sections.title: Pokédex entries
sections.text: Pokédex entries [ edit | edit source ] Episode Pokémon Source Entry BW022 Venipede Ash's Pokédex Venipede, the Centipede Pokémon. Venipede explores its surroundings by sensing vibrations in the air with feelers on its head and tail.
